varieties.name: STANDARD LAWSONIA INERMIS
varieties.traits: Species type, open-pollinated; Multi-branched shrub 2-6 meters, glossy dark green elliptical leaves 2-5cm long
varieties.yield_notes: Lower than tropical regions; 200-500 kg/ha dry leaves (vs 1000-1500 in optimal zones)
varieties.growing_notes: Available from Australian native plant nurseries. Requires full sun, well-drained soil. in humid subtropical climates, grow in large containers that can be moved to warmest microclimate in winter. Prune after leaves yellow in cooler months. Plant tolerates Humid subtropical humidity but produces less dye than in hotter climates.
varieties.availability: Daleys Fruit Trees (Queensland), Mudbrick Herb Cottage, All Rare Herbs, The Seed Vine (Australian nurseries)

varieties.name: DESI TYPE
varieties.traits: Traditional landrace, open-pollinated; Leafy canopy with larger green leaves, less thorny
varieties.yield_notes: Better leaf yield potential; adapted to monsoon dry-wet cycles
varieties.growing_notes: Traditional Indian selection favored for commercial cultivation. in humid subtropical climates, may struggle with consistent year-round moisture vs. the dry-wet cycles it evolved with. Best grown in controlled water regime - allow soil to dry between heavy waterings. Frost-tender; requires winter protection.
varieties.availability: Specialty seed suppliers; cuttings may be sourced from Indian plant importers (subject to quarantine regulations)

varieties.name: MURALIYA TYPE
varieties.traits: Traditional landrace, open-pollinated; Woody canopy, small greyish-green leaves, hard pointed thorn-like branchlets
varieties.yield_notes: Lower than Desi type; slower growing
varieties.growing_notes: More drought-tolerant and hardy than Desi type. The thorniness makes handling difficult. Not preferred for commercial cultivation but more resilient to stress. in humid subtropical climates, this hardiness may be advantageous during dry periods. Better suited to hedging than dye production.
varieties.availability: Rare in Australian trade; primarily available in India

varieties.name: STANDARD LAWSONIA INERMIS 🌱
varieties.origin: Native to tropical regions including India
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated species type; Multi-branched shrub 2-6 meters, dark green glossy leaves 2-5cm
varieties.yield_notes: 1000-1500 kg/ha dry leaves possible under rainfed conditions; 2000+ kg/ha with irrigation
varieties.growing_notes: HIGHLY RECOMMENDED FOR tropical monsoon climates. Thrives in monsoon climate with hot, humid conditions. Plant during February-March, transplant after monsoon arrives (June-July). Grows as hedge or field crop. Known locally as "Mailanchi" (Malayalam). Used traditionally for body art (mehndi), hair conditioning, and cooling properties. Extremely well-adapted to Tropical monsoon climate - better than most Indian regions.
varieties.availability: Local tropical monsoon climates nurseries, agricultural departments, traditional plant sellers

varieties.name: DESI TYPE (INDIAN LANDRACE) 🏆🌱
varieties.origin: Rajasthan, Gujarat, Madhya Pradesh - traditional Indian type
varieties.traits: Traditional landrace, open-pollinated, 100+ years cultivation; Leafy canopy, larger green leaves, minimal thorns, vigorous growth
varieties.yield_notes: High leaf yield potential 1500-2000 kg/ha in Tropical monsoon favorable climate

varieties.name: RAJASTHANI HENNA 🏆🌱
varieties.origin: Rajasthan, India (Pali, Sojat regions) - ancient cultivation area
varieties.traits: Regional landrace, open-pollinated, traditional variety; Adapted to arid regions but grows vigorously in tropical monsoon
varieties.yield_notes: Very high in monsoon climate - 1500-2500 kg/ha possible in tropical monsoon climates
varieties.growing_notes: Rajasthan is India's premier henna-producing region. While adapted to arid climate, this germplasm excels in Tropical monsoon monsoon conditions with adequate moisture. Higher quality dye than many other sources. Tropical monsoon temperature and humidity may produce even better yields than Rajasthan's arid climate. Traditional source of premium mehndi. Historical cultivation exceeds 400+ years.
varieties.availability: Sojat nurseries (Rajasthan), agricultural seed suppliers, can be sourced through Indian agricultural networks

varieties.name: SOJAT HENNA (GI TAGGED) 🏆🌱
varieties.origin: Sojat City, Pali District, Rajasthan (Geographical Indication Tag #628/21)
varieties.traits: Premium landrace with GI protection, open-pollinated; Fine texture leaves, rich green color, optimal lawsone content
varieties.yield_notes: In tropical monsoon climates climate: 2000-2500 kg/ha with proper management

varieties.name: PALI HENNA 🏆🌱
varieties.origin: Pali District, Rajasthan (22,000+ hectares under cultivation)
varieties.traits: Regional landrace, traditional open-pollinated; Vigorous growth, good leaf production
varieties.yield_notes: 2000+ kg/ha potential in Tropical monsoon favorable growing conditions

varieties.name: GUJARAT HENNA 🏆🌱
varieties.traits: Regional landrace, open-pollinated, traditional; High lawsone content, good leaf size and production

varieties.name: MARWARI HENNA 🏆🌱
varieties.origin: Marwar region, Rajasthan (historical name for Jodhpur area)
varieties.traits: Ancient landrace, open-pollinated, heritage variety; Drought-hardy genetics, produces well even under stress
varieties.yield_notes: 1000-1800 kg/ha; drought tolerance may not be primary advantage in tropical monsoon climates but hardiness is valuable

varieties.name: MADHYA PRADESH HENNA 🏆🌱
varieties.traits: Regional landrace, open-pollinated; Medium-sized shrub, good branching

varieties.name: TAMIL NADU HENNA 🌱
varieties.origin: Tamil Nadu, South India (neighboring state to tropical monsoon climates)
varieties.traits: Regional selection, open-pollinated; Adapted to South Indian tropical climate
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT CHOICE FOR tropical monsoon climates - Tamil Nadu shares similar climate characteristics with tropical monsoon climates. Known locally as "Maruthani" (similar to Malayalam "Maruthani"). This South Indian selection is already adapted to high humidity, monsoon patterns, and tropical heat. Likely the best genetic match for tropical monsoon climates conditions after local selections. Cultural significance in South Indian wedding traditions.
varieties.availability: Tamil Nadu nurseries, agricultural colleges, local plant sellers in South India

varieties.name: KERALA LOCAL SELECTION 🌱
varieties.origin: tropical monsoon climates State (backyard and traditional cultivation)
varieties.traits: Locally adapted population, open-pollinated; Selected over time for tropical monsoon climates climate; moderate growth 2-4 meters
varieties.growing_notes: BEST ADAPTED TO tropical monsoon climates - While not a commercial crop, henna has been grown in tropical monsoon climates backyards for generations for personal mehndi use. These local selections are perfectly adapted to Tropical monsoon specific rainfall patterns, humidity, and temperature ranges. Look for plants in traditional home gardens, ayurvedic gardens, or from elder community members who maintain "Mailanchi" plants. Most reliable for tropical monsoon climates region.
varieties.availability: Local tropical monsoon climates plant exchanges, traditional home gardens, ayurvedic plant nurseries, agricultural extension offices

varieties.name: AFRICAN HENNA
varieties.origin: North Africa (Egypt, Sudan, Morocco), East Africa (Somalia)
varieties.traits: African landraces, open-pollinated, ancient cultivation; Variable based on region; generally hardy and drought-tolerant

varieties.name: MOROCCAN VARIETIES
varieties.origin: Morocco (Alnif, Tafraoute Sidi Ali, Tazzarine regions)
varieties.yield_notes: Variable; potentially 1500-2000 kg/ha in tropical monsoon climates climate

varieties.name: EGYPTIAN HENNA 🏆🌱
varieties.traits: Ancient landrace, open-pollinated; Classical Egyptian type, historically significant
varieties.growing_notes: Egyptian henna has oldest documented use (3400 BCE - over 5000 years). Extreme historical and cultural significance. While adapted to Nile's dry climate with seasonal flooding, this pattern somewhat similar to monsoon cycles. Tropical monsoon year-round warmth advantageous. Ancient heirloom with unmatched historical pedigree. Traditional Egyptian privet designation.
varieties.availability: Specialty suppliers, botanical gardens, international seed sources (import permits required)

varieties.name: PAKISTANI/PUNJAB HENNA 🏆🌱
varieties.origin: Pakistan, Punjab region (shared with Indian Punjab)
varieties.traits: Traditional landrace, open-pollinated; Vigorous growth in subtropical monsoon climate

varieties.name: YEMENI HENNA
varieties.traits: Middle Eastern landrace, open-pollinated, ancient; Adapted to hot, arid conditions with some moisture
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate 800-1500 kg/ha; may increase in Tropical monsoon moisture
varieties.growing_notes: Yemen has ancient henna traditions, particularly for wedding ceremonies. Flowers especially fragrant and used in traditional perfumery. While from arid region, may adapt to tropical monsoon climates warmth. Ancient cultivation (1000+ years). Cultural significance in Arabian/Islamic traditions.
varieties.availability: Specialty Middle Eastern seed suppliers (import regulations apply)

varieties.name: BANGLADESHI HENNA 🌱
varieties.traits: Regional selection, open-pollinated; Adapted to monsoon tropical climate very similar to tropical monsoon climates
